ticket_title: Implement authn/authz for the network/cni isolator
ticket_description: Currently any framework can launch containers on any CNI network irrespective of its role and principal. subtask_title: Investigate the issue in 6.2.0.RC
subtask_reasoning: This subtask involves reproducing the issue and understanding its root cause, which is a critical step in resolving the bug.

ticket_title: Refactor and unify CoaddPsf, CoaddBoundedField, and CoaddTransmissionCurve
ticket_description: These components all do similar things but take different approaches to their implementation and public interface, making it hard to share much code between them.   * {{CoaddPsf}} uses an internal {{ExposureCatalog}}, which makes both its persisted and in-memory sizes larger than they need to be, but simplifies its persistence and evaluation code.  {{CoaddBoundedField}} and {{CoaddTransmissionCurve}} (still WIP on DM-12370) use a vector of custom structs to avoid this.   * {{CoaddPsf}} and {{CoaddBoundedField}} have public interfaces that are strongly tied to these implementation choices, while {{CoaddTransmissionCurve's}} implementation is entirely private (even the class itself is hidden behind a factory function).    All three classes should probably use a vector a shared object (not {{ExposureCatalog}}, but also not vectors of custom structs) to consolidate persistence and lookup code.  They should also probably all be hidden behind private factory functions or at least take a consistent approach to this question that does not expose their implementations.
subtask_title: Refactor CoaddPsf to use a vector of shared objects
subtask_reasoning: This subtask involves changing the internal ExposureCatalog to a vector of shared objects, simplifying persistence and evaluation code.

ticket_title: Improve EL API considering feedback
ticket_description: We need to take into account Achaval's feedback regarding the EL API and make the appropriate modifications. His thoughts:  {quote}  ValidationResult      - Needs to have a list of messages           -  A message has               - severity: Severity (Warning,Error,Info)              - message: String              - location: Location                  - startPosition: Position (line:Int,column:Int,offset:Int)                 - endPosition:Position   Nice to have two factory methods   - createSucessResult   - createErrorResult       Nice to have a factory method with an already existing bindingContext specially to start summing context  BindingContextBuild.builder(bindingContext)    ScriptingException? What type of exception should I throw when it fails the execution.    evaluate(script: String, expectedOutputType: DataType, bindingContext: BindingContext)  For this to work we need to discuss */* what is the menaing of this?? Should it be changed to application/java -- (Pablo K and Pablo LG)  Also need a way to access the mule transformers  {quote}    Seems like we need to:   - provide a better ValidationResult abstraction and a factory (basic factory methods added in (done in MULE-10842))   - provide an exception signature for the evaluate method   - provide a factory method for the binding context considering an existing one (done in MULE-10842)   - handle expectedDataType stuff ourselves (in the ExpressionLanguage instead of the ExpressionExecutor) since we have access to the MuleContext and all transformers (done in MULE-10842)
subtask_title: Improve ValidationResult abstraction
subtask_reasoning: This subtask involves creating a better ValidationResult abstraction and adding factory methods for creating success and error results.

ticket_title: Improve performance of appc -v command
ticket_description: When the registry server is not reachable, or slow to respond, then {{appc -v}} command takes a long time to execute, and print package's version. Since it is just about printing the local package version, the availability of registry server shouldn't impact on {{appc -v}} command's performance.
subtask_title: Implement local version caching for appc -v command
subtask_reasoning: This subtask involves implementing a local cache for the package version, which will improve performance when the registry server is not reachable.

ticket_title: iOS Debugger: Breakpoint not hit for Alloy applications
ticket_description: During debug on iOS of an Alloy application the breakpoints are not hit.     This is a regression on SDK as the issue happens with the 6.0 SDK but does not happen with 5.5.1.GA version of SDK    * The issue happens only for Alloy apps. For classic apps the breakpoints are hit successfully  * The issue occurs on both device and simulator  * This issue was found while testing the debug with TiCore framework     Steps to reproduce issue  1. Create a new alloy project  2. Add a breakpoint  3. Debug the project on iOS device or sim    Expected Results  The breakpoints are hit    Actual Results  The breakpoints are not hit
subtask_title: Reproduce the issue on a clean Alloy project
subtask_reasoning: This subtask involves creating a new Alloy project, adding a breakpoint, and debugging the project on iOS to reproduce the issue.

ticket_title: Remove seqNum from obs_lsst/ts8 and prepare obs_lsst for bootcamp
ticket_description: Test stand data has a totally different definition for the sequence number - it's the number within a sequence (Fe55, flats, darks, etc) and there are many sequences per day, hence this number is not unique within a day like it might be elsewhere.    It therefore can't be used with the dayObs for identifying exposures, and will be totally removed, and replaced with visit numbers.    Also, apply other fixes to necessary for bootcamp to run.
subtask_title: Remove seqNum from obs_lsst/ts8
subtask_reasoning: This subtask requires the removal of seqNum from obs_lsst/ts8, which is a relatively simple task.

ticket_title: Time Series viewer: image issues
ticket_description: I. Here are some test cases that do not behave as expected.  (lc_raw.tbl from WISE and LSST_ 2990683841366709.tbl from LSST are attached)    1. http://localhost:8080/firefly/ts.html  2. Upload WISE lc_raw.tbl, wait for 5 images to load  3. Upload LSST_2990683841366709.tbl, wait for 3 images to load. (The first image appears rotated 34 degrees)    1. http://localhost:8080/firefly/ts.html  2. Upload a raw table, wait for images to load  3. Change cutout size to 0.05 (all images reloaded, the first image appears with much smaller zoom then the others)    1. http://localhost:8080/firefly/ts.html  2. Upload WISE lc_raw.tbl, wait for 5 images to load  3. Select  5th row (5th image becomes middle, two more are loaded on the right)  4. Change cutout size to 0.05 (all images are reloaded)  5. Select 1st row (second image appears with larger zoom, taking all viewer space, while others appear small    1. http://localhost:8080/firefly/ts.html  2. Upload WISE lc_raw.tbl, wait for 5 images to load  3. Choose rotate North (all images are rotated)  4. Select  5th row (5th image becomes middle, two more are loaded on the right - these do not appear rotated north and Target Match button gets unchecked)    II. In Safari Version 9.1.3, images do not appear in Expanded View. (Chrome is fine.)
subtask_title: Fix image rotation issue in Time Series viewer
subtask_reasoning: This subtask involves fixing the image rotation issue in the Time Series viewer, which is a specific and well-defined problem.

ticket_title: Build: Update, Package and Deploy the dependencies
ticket_description: Unified CLI has a lot of dependencies/modules, and if a few of those modules are updated, then there is a chance that we are missing to include those updated modules in new version of appc-cli.    When we attempt to publish a new version of appc-cli, the proposal is -    - We know a list of dependencies (appc-cli-titanium, arrow, appc-platform-sdk...)  - Look for any code changes (in git) after the latest tag, or any changes after the recent version of appc-cli published date.  - For each modified dependent module,   -- Update the version  -- Publish the npm package  -- Tag a release in the specified branch (4_0_X or 4_1_X)  - Change the package.json of appc-cli with all of the modified module versions.  - Publish appc-cli.  - Tag appc-cli version in the github with the release name.
subtask_title: Update dependencies in appc-cli
subtask_reasoning: This subtask involves updating the package.json of appc-cli with the modified module versions.

ticket_title: Set up development environment on NCSA OpenStack
ticket_description: A development playground (multi-node, consisting of czar, several workers, and some test data) is needed for prototyping data distribution and replication.  It is anticipated that this will be a non-containerized deployment on NCSA OpenStack VMs in order to facilitate rapid development.    The general plan for this effort includes the following steps:  # create and configure 1 *master* and 10 *worker* nodes  # external access to the cluster via the *Floating IP* of the *master* node  # user accounts and security: create user *qserv*, configure *ssh*, etc.  # allocate 11 volumes (one per node) for data and databases  # setup an NFS server on *master* to export some local folder to all workers for sharing *LSST* Stack and other relevant *LSST* packages  # setup the minimally required *RPM*s to support the software deployment and development efforts  # build a local copy of the *LSST* stack and other relevant package on the exported volume  # configure and start *Qserv* (using *Docker* container) on all nodes  # *IMPORTANT*: make a snapshot of the VMs of all instances  # import a data set (a subset of chunks of PDAC's *wise* or *stripe82*)
subtask_title: Create and configure master and worker nodes
subtask_reasoning: This task involves creating and configuring 11 nodes, which is a significant portion of the overall effort.

ticket_title: Neither formchangechecker, nor quiz autosave detects changes in Atto editors

It seems that it they will alos need special code to work with Atto, but no-one has written that.    To reproduce (Quiz)  # (Optional) Add $CFG->forced_plugin_settings = array('quiz' => array('autosaveperiod' => 3)); to your config.php to make thes easier to test. (Just a 3 second delay between a change and an auto-save.)  # Ensure you have a default set-up using Atto, not TinyMCE or another editor.  # Create a quiz containing one essay question, that uses the 'HTML editor with file picker' response type.  # Attempt the quiz, and Open the console. (The autosave code outputs useful debug messages to tell you what is going on - assuming you have developer debug on.)  # Type something in Atto, and verify that 3 seconds later (or whatever delay you set) an autosave ajax request happens.  # Add an image to Atto using the filepicker, and verify that cause an autosave too.    To reproduce (Formchangechecker)  # Go to a form, and click to make a new post.  # Type an X in the message box.  # Click the back button. You should get a warning that you are about to leave a page with unsaved data, but you don't.

subtask_reasoning: This subtask involves implementing the special code required for Atto autosave, which is a key part of the ticket.

ticket_title: Android: allow users to launch x86 emulator
ticket_description: h2. Description    Developers need to be able to leverage the Android x86 emulator that was made available with Android SDK r17. It claims to have performance increases beyond the standard emulator making for a better development experience.    h2. Proposed Solution(s)    It would be good if you could specify by default whether you want to use the x86 or standard emulator for all projects, perhaps in {{Preferences -> Titanium Studio -> Titanium}}. It should be the standard emulator by default as the x86 emulator currently does not include the Google APIs (which would prevent the KitchenSink from running) and it does not support V8, as detailed in the linked ticket.    Also, it should be configurable on a per-project basis as well, for the same reasons I listed in the above paragraph.    So:    # We need to upgrade our AVI detection to show the available ABIs per device  # For devices with multiple ABIs we need to allow the user to select which one to use as part of the launch configuration, and that is passed as the first argument to the SDK for SDKs 2.1+. For < 2.1, we only pass the device argument  # We could add a preference in Titanium Preferences to default to x86 or standard emulator (were available). This would se the default ABI selection in the launch configuration.
subtask_title: Upgrade AVI detection to show available ABIs per device
subtask_reasoning: This subtask requires modifying the AVI detection to show the available ABIs per device, which is a relatively simple task.

ticket_title: Parity: Ti.Filesystem.getFile inconsistent in support of various URIs across platforms
ticket_description: Between iOS and Android, we have very inconsistent support for various URIs we can pass in to refer to files. Some of this is expected, as Android has platform specific {{content:}}, {{appdata-private:}}, {{android.resource:}}, and {{file:///android_asset}} schemes.    However, there's many differences in handling {{file:}} URIs, {{app:}} URIs, and paths with no scheme.    Specifically in calling {{Ti.Filesystem.getFile()}}:  - iOS does not seem to support using {{app:}} URIs. (A workaround would be to call {{Ti.App.appURLToPath()}} on it first? Which is an undocumented public API!)  - Android doesn't support {{file://}} or {{file:}} URIs for relative paths and assumes/forces absolute  - iOS can't handle {{file:}} URIs (no trailing double-slash on the scheme)  - Android assumes {{appdata-private:}} scheme implicitly if not scheme is provided    See the test suite here: https://github.com/<USER>titanium_mobile/blob/6f176428755144db135986b281d08430fd036692/tests/Resources/ti.filesystem.file.addontest.js
subtask_title: Implement parity for file: URIs on iOS
subtask_reasoning: This subtask requires implementing parity for file: URIs on iOS, similar to TIMOB-27322.

ticket_title: Implement direct gradients in scarlet
ticket_description: Currently scarlet uses autograd to calculated the gradients used for optimization. This creates an overhead in both memory and runtime, as autograd requires a wrapper for all functions used in the code in order to back-propagate the gradients for each variable.    For scarlet we can calculate almost all of those gradients ourselves, eliminating the need to use autograd. The attached notebook demonstrates how this can be done, giving an outline of the runtime differences between autograd and direct gradients using a simplified modeling algorithm with a single back-propagation. It also demonstrates that gradients can be back-propagated up to 10 times faster per iteration when using gradients that we calculate ourselves, where the exact saving is based on the ratio of the PSF to the full size image (which translates into how much time we are spending on gradient calculation vs convolutions).    DM-23788 implements convolutions in real space, resulting in a model that allows us to calculate all of the gradients directly ourselves. However, it also demonstrates that real space convolutions are slower than those performed in k-space, so we are likely to use fft convolutions in production. We could in principle still use autograd to calculate the FFT convolution gradients for us, however since we only need the gradients for that single operation it will likely be easier to just implement a direct gradient for the FFTs as well (which may be as simple as calling a function in autograd).
subtask_title: Implement direct gradients for FFT convolutions
subtask_reasoning: This subtask is estimated to be 8 story points, as it involves implementing a direct gradient for the FFT convolution operation, which may be as simple as calling a function in autograd.

ticket_title: Deploy replication service at PDAC
ticket_description: Major milestones of this effort:  # upgrade Qserv installation in PDAC with the latest version of Docker containers based on MariaDB 10.2.14. The new containers are also needed to support the extended management protocol needed for cooperation between Qserv workers and the Replication system's Controllers when changes to replica disposition are made.  # Docker-based install and preliminary tests of the Replication system's tools on the cluster. At this stage a proper configuration of the Replication system will be devised and tested.  # Scalability tests of the main replication operations, including operations with persistent state of the operations (Replication _jobs_ and _requests_). Making improvements to the implementation of the system as needed.  # Implement the _Health Monitoring Algorithm_ for workers of both kinds (Qserv and the Replication system). Integrate this algorithm into the above mentioned _fixed logic Controller_. The initial version of the algorithm will be sending _probe_ requests to both kinds of workers and measure their responses (which must arrive within a reasonable period of time). This may also require to make some adjustments to the Replication system's Messaging Network to respect priorities of these requests. The current implementation has a plain queue. The new one should get a priority queue.  # Finalize and test the fixed-logic replication Controller  # when confident with the functionality, performance and robustness of the Replication system integrate the system with the existing Kubernetes infrastructure
subtask_title: Upgrade Qserv installation in PDAC
subtask_reasoning: This task involves upgrading Qserv installation to the latest version of Docker containers based on MariaDB 10.2.14.

ticket_title: Define API for Stack Astrometric Calibration
ticket_description: 70% RO 30% KSK This should also include a minimal implementation.  This should be done with an eye toward photometric calibration.  Prerequisite: Get multi epoch (multi-band?) catalogs of centroids from some trusted source (CFHT, HSC?).  1. Load all stars that overlap a patch for all epochs. 2. Associate all stars on each chip.   2a. Implement K-D tree 3. Fit model for rigid chip system + optics + atmosphere.  Eigen for sparse model fitter.   3a. Allow for external catalog.   3b. This could include a class to fit XYTransforms 4. Turn result into WCS.  So maybe a down scope for a single cycle epic is to get matches and interfaces for models and solvers.
